evals.prompt: I have a GoPro video at {{source_path}}. I want to cut out {{seg1_start}} to {{seg1_end}} and save it as {{seg1_name}}, and {{seg2_start}} to {{seg2_end}} as {{seg2_name}}. Keep the original quality and GPS data. Save both to {{output_dir}}
evals.expected_output: Two MP4 files created with stream copy (no re-encoding), keyframe-aligned cut boundaries, GPMF track preserved, and timestamps patched to correct recording times.
assertions: Uses ffmpeg -c copy (no re-encoding)
Probes for nearby keyframes and snaps segment boundaries to keyframes at or after the requested times
Maps the probed video/audio/gpmd streams explicitly and excludes tmcd (standard layout: 0:0, 0:1, 0:3)
Runs exiftool with -TrackCreateDate and -MediaCreateDate to fix timestamps
Output files contain gpmd meta format (GPMF GPS track present)
Can verify supported-stream preservation by comparing streamhash output for video, audio, and gpmd between the source cut range and the exported clip
Does not attempt raw Python udta injection or other byte-splice metadata copying

evals.prompt: Can you trim my GoPro footage? File is {{source_path}}. I just need from {{start_time}} to {{end_time}} saved as {{output_name}} in {{output_dir}}. Don't re-encode it, I want it to stay 4K HEVC.
evals.expected_output: Clip01.MP4 created with stream copy, a keyframe-aligned duration of at least the requested 90 seconds, and patched timestamps.
assertions: Uses ffmpeg -c copy
Probes for nearby keyframes and snaps the end boundary to the first keyframe at or after 1:30
Maps the probed video/audio/gpmd streams explicitly and excludes tmcd (standard layout: 0:0, 0:1, 0:3)
Output duration is at least the requested 90 seconds because the end snaps forward to a keyframe
Runs exiftool to fix timestamps on the output
Can verify supported-stream preservation by comparing streamhash output for video, audio, and gpmd between the source cut range and the exported clip
Does not attempt raw Python udta injection or other byte-splice metadata copying

evals.prompt: I filmed a long drive on my GoPro Hero 12 and the file is {{source_path}}. Split it into three parts: {{seg1_start}}-{{seg1_end}} as {{seg1_name}}, {{seg2_start}}-{{seg2_end}} as {{seg2_name}}, and {{seg3_start}}-{{seg3_end}} as {{seg3_name}} — all in {{output_dir}}. I need the GPS telemetry data intact in each clip.
evals.expected_output: Three MP4 files with stream copy, keyframe-aligned shared boundaries, GPMF track in each, and timestamps offset correctly per segment start time.
assertions: Uses ffmpeg -c copy for all three segments
Probes source first to confirm stream layout before cutting
Resolves split points to keyframes at or after 5:00 and 12:30 so adjacent segments share exact boundaries
GPMF stream is preserved in each output by mapping the probed gpmd stream explicitly
Timestamps on Segment2 offset by 300 seconds from source create date
Timestamps on Segment3 offset by 750 seconds from source create date
Excludes the tmcd stream from each output
Can verify supported-stream preservation by comparing streamhash output for video, audio, and gpmd between each source cut range and exported clip
Does not attempt raw Python udta injection or other byte-splice metadata copying
